Title: The Jasmonate-Responsive SmMPK3–SmWRKY33 Module Positively Regulates Tanshinone Biosynthesis in Salvia miltiorrhiza

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: major bioactive diterpenes used to treat cardiovascular and cerebrovascular diseases
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: JAZ repressor protein; contains Jas motif and EAR motif
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: methyl jasmonate (MeJA) treatment; MG132 proteasome inhibitor
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H; LCI; pull-down; Co-IP; dual-LUC; EMSA; MeJA-induced degradation assay
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: SmCPS1; SmKSL1; SmCYP76AH3; SmCYP71D373; Sm2-ODD14 promoters in dual-LUC assays
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: functions as JA-signalling repressor in Danshen; protein degraded after MeJA treatment
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SmJAZ8 physically interacts with SmWRKY33 through its Jas motif. 2. SmJAZ8 represses SmWRKY33-dependent activation of SmCPS1, SmKSL1, SmCYP76AH3, SmCYP71D373 and Sm2-ODD14. 3. SmJAZ8 reduces SmWRKY33 DNA-binding ability, and MeJA-triggered degradation partially relieves this repression.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: represses tanshinone biosynthesis by attenuating SmWRKY33 activity; MeJA-induced degradation alleviates repression
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: EAR motif is required for strong repression; Jas motif is required for interaction with SmWRKY33; targeted for 26S proteasome-dependent degradation upon MeJA treatment
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of MeJA-treated hairy roots, CNCB PRJCA038900
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Relieving SmJAZ8 repression, e.g. knockout/editing or combining with SmWRKY33/SmMPK3 activation, may enhance tanshinone production

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: major bioactive diterpenes used to treat cardiovascular and cerebrovascular diseases
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Group I WRKY transcription factor with two WRKY domains, Ser/Pro-rich cluster, D domain, proline-rich N-terminus, C-terminal activation domain
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates SmCPS1, SmKSL1, SmCYP76AH3, SmCYP71D373 and Sm2-ODD14 by binding W-box promoter elements; indirectly upregulates additional pathway genes including SmGGPPS1, SmCYP76AH1, SmCYP76AK1 and SmCYP71D375
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: key positive JA-responsive regulator coordinating early and late tanshinone pathway genes
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: MeJA; repression by SmJAZ8; activation by SmMPK3-mediated phosphorylation
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: forms inhibitory complex with SmJAZ8; interacts with SmMPK3 kinase
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: overexpression; SRDX dominant repression; dual-LUC; Y1H; EMSA; Y2H; LCI; pull-down; Co-IP; metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: JA-induced; root-preferential; highest in periderm and also detected in xylem
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SmWRKY33 is a JA-induced Group I WRKY highly expressed in root periderm. 2. Overexpression increases major tanshinones, while SRDX repression strongly decreases them. 3. SmWRKY33 directly binds and activates SmCPS1, SmKSL1, SmCYP76AH3, SmCYP71D373 and Sm2-ODD14 promoters. 4. SmJAZ8 represses SmWRKY33 activity, whereas SmMPK3 phosphorylation enhances its DNA binding and transactivation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes tanshinone accumulation, including DHT, tanshinone I, cryptotanshinone and tanshinone IIA
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: phosphorylation-deficient SmWRKY33-5A shows reduced DNA binding and transactivation; phosphomimetic SmWRKY33-5D shows enhanced DNA binding; no effect of these mutations on localization or protein interactions
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of MeJA-treated hairy roots, CNCB PRJCA038900
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV35S for stable overexpression in S. miltiorrhiza; pSuper for transient assays
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for metabolic engineering to enhance tanshinone production, especially combined with relief of JAZ repression or kinase activation

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: roots/hairy roots; enriched in root periderm-associated pathway context
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: major bioactive diterpenes used to treat cardiovascular and cerebrovascular diseases
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: mitogen-activated protein kinase; homolog of Arabidopsis MPK3
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: enhances SmWRKY33-mediated activation of SmCPS1 and other tanshinone biosynthetic genes indirectly via phosphorylation of SmWRKY33; overexpression upregulates SmGGPPS1, SmCPS1, SmKSL1, SmCYP76AH3, SmCYP71D373, SmCYP71D375 and SmWRKY33
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: positive JA-responsive kinase that boosts SmWRKY33 activity and thereby increases tanshinone biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: co-expression analysis; RT-qPCR; Y2H; LCI; pull-down; Co-IP; in vitro kinase assay; LC-MS/MS phosphosite identification; in vivo phosphorylation assay; dual-LUC; EMSA; hairy root overexpression; metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: enriched in xylem and periderm; induced by MeJA
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: induced at 0.5 h after MeJA; enriched in xylem and periderm
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SmMPK3 is a MeJA-responsive MAP kinase co-expressed with SmWRKY33. 2. SmMPK3 directly interacts with and phosphorylates SmWRKY33, mainly at T53, S78 and S90 within the SP cluster. 3. Phosphorylation enhances SmWRKY33 DNA-binding and transcriptional activation. 4. SmMPK3 overexpression in hairy roots increases tanshinone pathway gene expression and accumulation of major tanshinones.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: positively regulates tanshinone accumulation through phosphorylation-dependent activation of SmWRKY33
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Unexpected reduction of Sm2-ODD14 transcript in SmMPK3-overexpressing hairy roots despite increased overall tanshinone accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: interaction requires N-terminus of SmWRKY33 and C-terminus of SmMPK3; phosphorylation-deficient SmWRKY33-5A responds weakly to SmMPK3
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of MeJA-treated hairy roots, CNCB PRJCA038900
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pK7WG2D overexpression construct for stable hairy root transformation; pSuper for transient assays
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful positive regulator for engineering higher tanshinone yield, particularly in combination with SmWRKY33 activation
